signals.title: USD/JPY Watch
signals.text: Watch for USD/JPY volatility as WTI crude oil closed at $102.5 on Friday, down 2.45%, on hopes of resumed US-Iran negotiations

signals.title: EUR/USD Setup
signals.text: Watch for EUR/USD at the open, given EUR/USD Friday close: 1.1723 and Trump's announcement of increased tariffs on European autos

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Expectations
signals.text: Watch for AUD/USD, with RBA hiking expectations and AUD/USD Friday close: 0.7208, as AUD has a carry advantage of +335bp over JPY

signals.title: JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield
signals.text: JPY outperformed USD by 1.953% this week, with JPY 1W avg: +1.081% and USD 1W avg: -0.872%, and a US 10Y yield of 4.39%

signals.title: BoJ — This Week
signals.text: The next BoJ meeting is the key directional catalyst for USD/JPY, with BoJ rate at 0.75% and USD/JPY Friday close: 157.03

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential continues to favor the USD, yet the pair's fundamental backdrop is becoming increasingly interesting. Specs are short -16.8k contracts, while the RBNZ is signaling a hawkish bias with a 40% hike priced in. This divergence sets up a short-squeeze risk, as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signaling. We see this dynamic coming to a head at the next RBNZ meeting on 27 May.
